content: If we map the linguistic learning understanding of "reflexion" (as reflection on one’s own writing and language use) onto the LLM iterative improvement framework of Self-Refine, we can design a human-aligned LLM fine-tuning paradigm that mimics how high school students refine their expressive writing through structured self-reflection exercises, rather than only correcting for logical or task errors. This paradigm prioritizes iterative alignment to natural human expressive norms over just task performance improvement.
